zh.lifeSummary: Per Eriksson於1520年9月11日出生在瑞典亞特藍德利特索。他於大約1545年與Valborg Mattsdotter在瑞典亞特藍德利特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1601年在他的家鄉享年81歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Per Eriksson于1520年9月11日出生在瑞典亞特藍德利特索。他于大约1545年与Valborg Mattsdotter在瑞典亞特藍德利特结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1601年在他的家乡享年81岁。

nameMeanings.name: Eriksson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Nils, Per, Alf, Algot, Alvar, Bjorn, Elof, Knut, Mats.
Swedish: patronymic from the personal name Erik, from Old Norse Eiríkr (see Erickson ). Compare Erickson , Ericsson , and Erikson .

variants.name: Erikson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Anders, Nils, Astrid, Berndt, Iver, Karsten, Lars, Lennart, Lief, Thor.  German Gerhart, Ernst.

Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish   Eriksson  . This is also an Americanized form of the Norwegian, Danish, and North German cognate   Eriksen  . Compare   Erekson   and   Erickson  .

variants.name: Erickson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Nels, Thor, Nils, Lars, Sven, Lief, Anders, Lennart, Iver, Elof, Erland.

Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Ericsson and Eriksson , Norwegian, Danish, and North German Eriksen and Erichsen , or of any other Scandinavian or North German patronymic from a personal name derived from Old Norse Eiríkr, which is composed of ei ‘ever, always’ (or a shortened form of ein ‘one, only’) + rík ‘power’. Compare Ericksen , Ericson , and Erikson .
English (Lancashire): patronymic from the Middle English male personal name Eric (with the same etymology as 1 above) + son.

variants.name: Erekson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Thor.
Americanized form of Swedish Eriksson or of any other Scandinavian patronymic from a personal name derived from Old Norse Eiríkr (see Erickson ).

variants.name: Ericsson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Lars, Sven, Anders, Britt, Gunvor, Johan, Kerstin, Tor.
Swedish: variant of   Eriksson  . Compare   Erickson   and   Ericson  .

nameMeanings.name: Per
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Swedish War of Liberation
historicEvents.description: The Swedish War of Liberation was a civil war led by nobleman Gustav Vasa. As Christian II heard of the rebellion, he confronted Vasa's men, who won the battle at Brunbacks Ferry. Christian II was defeated by the Swedes in Stockholm and a truce was agreed upon. Vasa was given as a hostage as a show of good faith until the treaty was signed. The Danish King did not keep his word and Vasa was held as a prisoner for two years until his escape. A bounty was placed on Vasa's head. Vasa worked to inspire many to join his cause and lived under disguise until the Danes surrendered in 1525.

historicEvents.title: Treaty of Malmo
historicEvents.description: The Treaty of Malmo was signed on September 1, 1524, which ended the Swedish War of Liberation and the independence of Sweden was recognized by Denmark and Norway.

historicEvents.title: Sweden Becomes Lutheran Nation
historicEvents.description: Sweden officially declared itself a Lutheran nation in 1544.
Peace of Stettin concluded. Denmark recognized independence of Sweden. Sweden gave up claim to Norway.

en.lifeSummary: Per Eriksson was born on 11 September 1520, in Söre, Lit, Jämtland, Sweden. He married Valborg Mattsdotter about 1545, in Lit, Jämtland, Sweden. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1601, in his hometown, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Per Eriksson est né le 11 septembre 1520, à Söre, Lit, Jämtland, Suède. Il a épousé Valborg Mattsdotter environ 1545, à Lit, Jämtland, Suède. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1601, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 81 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Per Eriksson wurde am 11. September 1520 in Söre, Lit, Jämtland, Schweden geboren. Er hat um 1545 in Lit, Jämtland, Schweden Valborg Mattsdotter geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1601 im Alter von 81 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Per Eriksson è nato il il 11 settembre 1520 nel Söre, Lit, Jämtland, Svezia. Lui sposato Valborg Mattsdotter circa 1545, nel Lit, Jämtland, Svezia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1601, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 81 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Per Erikssonは1520年9月11日にスウェーデンジaムトランドリトスoアで生まれた。約1545年にスウェーデンジaムトランドリトでValborg Mattsdotterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1601年に故郷で81歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Per Eriksson(은)는 1520년 9월 11일 스웨덴 ja틀랜드 릿 스o르에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1545년 스웨덴 ja틀랜드 릿에서 Valborg Mattsdotter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1601년 그의 고향에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Per Eriksson nasceu em 11 de setembro de 1520, em Söre, Lit, Jämtland, Suécia. Ele casou-se com Valborg Mattsdotter aproximadamente 1545, em Lit, Jämtland, Suécia. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1601, em sua cidade natal, com 81 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Per Eriksson родился 11 сентября 1520 г. в местности сoр, лит, джaмтланд, Швеция. Он женился на женщине по имени Valborg Mattsdotter около 1545 г. в местности лит, джaмтланд, Швеция. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1601 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 81 год.

es.lifeSummary: Per Eriksson nació el 11 de septiembre de 1520, en Söre, Lit, Jämtland, Suecia. Se casó con Valborg Mattsdotter aproximadamente 1545, en Lit, Jämtland, Suecia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1601, en su pueblo, a la edad de 81 años.
